wwilp|Landau's Problemswwigrwwilegwwin2p1wwitp|wwiabc|wwirh

Recent papers

Recent papers on the n^2+1 prime conjecture, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Skolem Meets Bateman-HornFlorian Luca, James Maynard, Armand Noubissie, Joël Ouaknine, James Worrell2023-08#2
2Primitive root bias for twin primes II: Schinzel-type theorems for totient quotients and the sum-of-divisors functionStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Kye Shi, Gabe Udell2019-06#2
3Primitive root biases for prime pairs I: existence and non-totality of biasesStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Timothy Schaaff2017-08#2
4Primitive root bias for twin primesStephan Ramon Garcia, Elvis Kahoro, Florian Luca2017-05#2
5Heuristics of the Cocks-Pinch methodMin Sha2012-11#6
6Möbius cancellation on polynomial sequences and the quadratic Bateman-Horn conjecture over function fieldsWill Sawin, Mark Shusterman2020-08#14
7Bateman-Horn, polynomial Chowla and the Hasse principle with probability 1Tim Browning, Efthymios Sofos, Joni Teräväinen2022-12#19
8Schinzel Hypothesis on average and rational pointsAlexei N. Skorobogatov, Efthymios Sofos2020-05#19
9Cyclotomic polynomials with prescribed height and prime number theoryAlexandre Kosyak, Pieter Moree, Efthymios Sofos, Bin Zhang2019-10#19
10Rational points and prime values of polynomials in moderately many variablesKevin Destagnol, Efthymios Sofos2018-01#19
11Serre's problem on the density of isotropic fibres in conic bundlesEfthymios Sofos2016-02#19
12Estimating the tail of the singular product in the multivariate Bateman-Horn conjectureVictor Volfson2026-04#24
13Probabilistic analytical approach to determining the asymptotics of prime objects on the initial interval of the natural seriesVictor Volfson2015-06#24
14Contradictions in some primes conjecturesVictor Volfson2014-07#24
15Counting conjugacy classes of subgroups of ${\rm PSL}_2(p)$Gareth A. Jones2024-11#26

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwin2p1.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.
